intro: Medica inermis, commonly known as Prickly Ash or Zanthoxylum clavatum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and treating digestive issues, due to its ability to stimulate the digestive system and improve circulation. The bioactive constituents of Medica inermis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Medica inermis include tinctures, teas, and infusions, as well as topical applications such as creams and salves.
